results.title: superwhisper Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

 84 views

•4 reviews

Literally use it daily to communicate with AI. So helpful for a world currently where boundaries of capability varies across different frontier models depending on the problem you have in front of you. This tool completely opened up a brand new way of me being able to interact with AI - effectively allowing for me to talk 10 minutes to it at a time in one input, and having the AI have all the context it needs to either 1. help me with the problem/situation at hand, 2. reflect back to me what is it I'm missing or 3. provide value to me simply as a means of organizing and refining my thoughts via speaking. Absolutely love it and the UI is my biggest inspiration right now across all the products I've grown to use and love the past few months. Super sleek. [...] Report

 14 views

•1 review

Such useful software! I find myself writing more and better documentation and emails with it because it makes text entry so fast. I'm more talkative and engaged in Slack too because I can speak and explain my thoughts well. The developer is always improving it and taking feedback. My partner, who has days of bad hand pain, uses it a lot too and is thankful for it.

#### What's great

fast performance (4)responsive developer (5)reduces typing (8)RSI relief (2)

Report

 15 views

Neil Chudleigh

superwhisper

superwhisper

Thanks Carl- makes me so happy to be building something that relieves pain.

Report

•2 reviews [...] #### What's great

AI integration (5)daily use (9)

Report

 19 views

•1 review

SuperWhisper has been a game changer for me. I'm able to move much faster as I work by using my voice instead of typing. I've also discovered that I process concepts better when I verbalize them. Dictating my communications with SuperWhisper has allowed me to do that in ways that I couldn't when I was typing more. The AI integration completely negates the feeling I used to have when using traditional dictation tools (ahem, Siri). I don't have to worry about getting everything said perfectly, nor even worry about having perfect grammar. Neil, the developer, is very responsive, open to suggestions, and welcomes critical feedback.

#### What's great

AI integration (5)responsive developer (5)reduces typing (8)

results.title: WHOOP: Your personal digital fitness and health coach | Product Hunt
results.content:
1

8

Whoop

Launched on September 2nd, 2014

1

11

Jai Ashar
Ян Петров
Dmitriy Pustovalov
Ян Петров

#### What's great

I had no idea how inefficient my sleep was until I saw the detailed breakdown of stages and the recommendations from the Sleep Coach. I'm also thrilled with the Journal feature: I was able to track how a late dinner or alcohol directly kills my recovery score - it really opened my eyes to my own habits. Within a few months, I've started waking up feeling much more rested and energetic. This is the best investment I've made in my health in recent years

#### What needs improvement

I would like to see tighter integration with meditation or mindfulness apps. It would be interesting to directly track how meditation affects HRV and recovery

#### vs Alternatives

results.title: Zzzappy: Science-backed breaks to protect your vision & prevent RSI | Product Hunt
results.content:
Mykola Kondratiuk

RSI prevention is underrated - wrist issues took out a few engineers on my team for weeks, costs more in lost output than any break schedule ever would. Curious how the timing works: is it adaptive based on input intensity, or fixed 20-20-20 style intervals?

张亦梓
Moocat ChatGPT input method

Moocat ChatGPT input method

@mykola\_kondratiuk Thank you — and totally agree, RSI prevention is often treated too late. Zzzappy uses adaptive timing based on real keyboard/mouse/trackpad load, so it’s not just a fixed 20-20-20 countdown. Heavy input ramps reminders sooner, while lighter work spaces them out more.

Mykola Kondratiuk [...] Zzzappy

Alex Ablazevics [...] Moocat ChatGPT input method

@thenomadcode Really appreciate this, especially from someone building focus tools. It tracks both active input load and idle gaps, so it’s not just raw volume over time. If you pause or step away, fatigue pressure drops instead of continuing like a dumb timer.

Letian Wang

Finally something that tracks actual input load instead of just a dumb timer. I code 8+ hours a day and my wrists only start hurting during heavy refactoring sessions, not when I'm reading docs. The frosted glass break screen is a nice touch too — way better than the ugly gray overlays other tools use.

张亦梓
